description:
When a `lib/` file defines multiple modules (an outer module plus inner
struct or helper modules), mutagen's site enumeration aborts with
`module_beam_missing`.

## Reproduction

From the atlas mutagen efficacy study (2026-05-18):

- File: `lib/atlas/kb/schema_registry.ex`
- Defines:
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry`
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.KindEntry` (nested)
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.RelTypeEntry` (nested)
- mutagen tries `:code.which(Elixir.KindEntry)` instead of
  `:code.which(Elixir.Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.KindEntry)`.
- Aborts because `Elixir.KindEntry` doesn't exist as a real BEAM atom.

## Why this matters

This is a common Phoenix/Ecto pattern:
- Controllers with nested `Embedded.Helpers`
- Ecto schemas with inner `Changesets`
- Context modules that group related struct modules in one file
- LiveView modules with inner `Components`

The current code assumes every `defmodule` is top-level. In a v1-only world
that was fine; the mutagen_ex codebase doesn't do this. Real apps do.

## Proposed fix

When walking a file's AST for module definitions, track the enclosing module
path. For nested `defmodule X do … defmodule Y do …` blocks, qualify `Y` as
`X.Y` before calling `:code.which/1`.

Implementation sketch:
- In `MutagenEx.Ast` (or wherever module discovery lives), thread a
  `parent_module` accumulator through the prewalk
- When a `defmodule Inner do …` AST node is encountered with a non-empty
  parent path, build the full atom `Elixir.<Parent>.<Inner>` before lookup

## Evidence

- Atlas study report (Finding 2 in TL;DR; failure-modes section)
- Cross-project synthesis Finding 4.1:
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-18-cross-project-synthesis.md

## Acceptance

- Mutation site enumeration succeeds on a multi-module file (test fixture
  with `defmodule A do ... defmodule A.B do ... end ... end`)
- `lib/atlas/kb/schema_registry.ex` runs without `module_beam_missing` when
  someone re-runs the atlas study
- Top-level-only files continue to work unchanged
title: Nested-module BEAM lookup uses non-qualified module name
